The best overall Prime Day 2026 deals on day 4
Apple AirPods Pro 3
Editor's note: These AirPods were $10 for a few days last week, but because they're 28% off and a NBC Select bestseller, we still think they're a good deal.

I’ve been a loyal AirPods Pro user for years. I use them to listen to music, take calls and while I’m doing workouts. This is Apple’s most recent model, with multiple listening modes (including noise cancellation) and up to 10 hours of battery life, according to the brand. The earbuds have sensors that track your heart rate and calories burned in workouts, a hearing aid feature and live language translation abilities. They come with ear tips in five sizes so you can get the best fit, and a Mag-Safe charging case for easy juicing up.
Cascade Platinum Plus Dishwasher Pods

Prime Day is an ideal time to save on household essentials like dishwasher pods, and Cascade’s made our list of the very best. You get 62 in a box — with the discount, that’s only about $0.30 per pod. Each one has soap inside that gets rid of grime, grease, sticky stuff and stuck-on food residue. NBC Select senior editor Shalwah Evans says the pods even get the worst residue off her air fryer rack and frying pans.
Samsonite Omni 2 Hardside Expandable Luggage

My Samsonite luggage is still in great condition after I’ve beat it up during years of travel. This set comes with two of the brand’s hardside Omni suitcases: a 20-inch carry-on and a 24-inch checked bag. They’re both expandable and have a scratch-resistant exterior, an adjustable handle, interior organization systems and 360-degree dual spinner wheels. There’s also a built-in TSA-approved luggage lock on the checked bag. Watch our Samsonite luggage review.
Dawn Platinum Powerwash Dish Spray (4-Pack)

As someone who doesn’t have a dishwasher, I’m very picky about my dish soap, and Dawn Powerwash is the only one I’ve bought for years. It gets literally everything off my dishes with minimal scrubbing and no water, rinses off in seconds and never leaves behind spots or a grimy residue. This starter pack comes with one sprayer and four refill bottles of soap.
Hefty Ultra Strong 13-Gallon Trash Bags
These 13-gallon, Fabuloso-scented trash bags are stretchable, leakproof and puncture-resistant, plus they have multi-day odor control and a drawstring closure. There are 110 bags in the box.
Dyson V8 Cordless Vacuum

Dyson makes some of our favorite cordless vacuums, including this lightweight one with a 40-minute run time. It has two cleaning modes, a built-in filter and a self-cleaning motorbar, plus it converts into a handheld vacuum. The vacuum comes with a crevice attachment and a wall dock for easy charging.
Premier Protein Shakes

Premier’s shelf-stable protein shakes come in disposable single-serve bottles, making them the ideal snack to take with you on the go. Each shake has 30 grams of protein per serving and is gluten-free, according to the brand. You can buy them in over a dozen flavors like chocolate, vanilla, cafe latte and caramel, many of which are on sale now.
Anker Nano Power Bank

This pocket-sized power bank has a built-in, foldable USB-C connector, so you can plug it into your phone, tablet or headphone case without a cord. To recharge the power bank, you can connect it directly to a block, or there’s a USB-C port if you want to use a cable.
Crest 3D Whitestrips Professional Effects

I’ve reported about oral care for six years, and no matter how many teeth whitening strips I test, Crest’s come out on top for two reasons: They actually work and they’re easy to use. The NBC Select Wellness Award-winning strips are coated in hydrogen peroxide to brighten your smile and remove surface stains, and they have a no-slip grip — I have conversations while wearing them and they don’t budge. You get 44 strips per pack, which is enough for 22 treatments.
Medicube Zero Pore Toner Pads 2.0
Use code MEDICUBEPD26 at checkout for an extra 10% off.

These exfoliating pore pads have transformed my skin over the past nine months — I use them every other night, and over time, they’ve made my skin softer and smoother, and visibly reduced pore size in my t-zone. The pads are soaked in an AHA and BHA solution, and they’re dual-sided: The textured side gently buffs away dead skin cells and the soft side has a soothing effect. Medicube’s Zero Pore Pads come with tweezers to grab one at a time, and there’s a spot to store the tool in the jar’s lid.
Amazon Kindle Paperwhite 16GB (Newest Model)

We named the (waterproof) Paperwhite the best Kindle after testing each Amazon e-reader: It has the longest battery life at about 12 weeks per charge, according to the brand, and you can adjust the tone of the screen light depending on where you’re reading to see pages better. The Paperwhite has a 7-inch glare-screen so you can read even in direct sunlight, and a compact size that’s ideal for travel or commuting. Read our Amazon Kindles review.
Waterpik Cordless Advanced 2.0 Flosser

This water flosser is an NBC Select Wellness Award-winner that also earned the American Dental Association’s Seal of Acceptance. It has three pressure settings and a water reservoir built into its handle that gives you about 45 seconds of continuous spray, according to the brand. The cordless, water-resistant Waterpik comes with four tips that rotate 360 degrees to reach all angles of your mouth. The brand says its battery lasts for up to four weeks per charge.
Shark NV356E Upright Vacuum Cleaner

This vacuum will be the cleaning hero in your home due to its powerful suction that cleans up messes on all floors. It also has a built-in HEPA filter that traps dust and other allergens. It also has a detachable pod, which lets you access hard-to-reach areas like under furniture and above doorframes. The vacuum comes with useful attachments like a dusting brush and a crevice tool to reach tight corners.
Amazon Smart Plug

Once you connect devices like lamps, fans and small kitchen appliances to this smart plug, you can control them from the Amazon Alexa app or via voice commands. The app also lets you set up timers, schedules and routines, plus the plug is compact, so it keeps your second outlet free.
Color Wow Dream Coat Supernatural Spray

Editor’s note: This product dropped to its Prime Day price for one day in April, but because it’s over 20% off, highly rated and a NBC Select staff and reader favorite product, we still think it’s a good deal.

“I’ve been using Color Wow Dream Coat for over three years and I don’t remember what it was like to blow dry my hair without it,” says NBC Select associate social media editor Caitlin Cusck. “I have super frizzy hair and this product makes a noticeable difference. It also leaves my hair feeling soft for days.” The spray’s anti-frizz formula blocks moisture and humidity to keep hair glossy and smooth, and it doubles as a heat protectant. It lasts through three to four washes, according to the brand, and Cusack says it’s very lightweight.
Tide Pods Liquid Laundry Detergent

There’s 76 Tide Pods in this pack, each of which is filled with laundry detergent that helps remove stains and odors from fabric, as well as maintains its color. The pods are HE-compatible and dissolve quickly, even in cold water, according to the brand.
Scrub Daddy BBQ Daddy Grill Brush

The BBQ Daddy is a bristle-free grill brush: There’s a replaceable, heat-resistant sponge on its head that you wet, and as you scrub the hot grates, it creates steam to loosen grease and food debris. The brush also has a built-in stainless steel scraper to get between grates and a retractable hook to lift grates up, plus a rotating handle and a bottle opener on the base.
Keurig K-Elite Single-Serve Coffee Maker

You can make hot and iced beverages in less than a minute with this Keurig single-serve coffee maker. There’s five cup sizes to choose from, and the appliance’s removable reservoir holds 75 ounces of water, so you don’t need to constantly refill it. It also has a strong brew setting if you like more intense flavor, and you can adjust settings like temperature and the auto on/off function. Plus, the Keurig’s drop tray is removable to accommodate tall travel mugs.
Hoto 4-in-1 Mini Cordless Handheld Car Vacuum

This handheld vacuum is much smaller than your typical dustbuster. It comes with six accessories, including a wide brush head for upholstery and narrow pointed crevice nozzle for hard-to-reach areas like dashboards and in between car seats. It weighs less than a pound, and has a run time of up to 30 minutes, plus it charges with a simple USB-C cable.
Jisulife Life7 Handheld Fan

You can use this as a neck fan with the included strap, a handheld fan by holding the base or a tabletop fan by folding the base down into a stand. It has five speed settings, which you can see on the fan’s LED display along with how much battery life is left. You get up to 19.5 hours of use per charge, according to the brand.
KIND Mini Snack Bars Variety Pack

These mini Kind bars are a quick and easy snack to throw in your bag or lunch box. They each have 90 calories or less and are made with gluten-free ingredients, according to the brand. You get 30 mini bars in this pack, and they come in three flavors: Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt, Caramel Almond & Sea Salt, and Dark Chocolate Cherry Cashew.
Worx WX242L 4V Lithium Screwdriver

Use this electric screwdriver to speed up building furniture, tackling repairs and doing DIY projects. It has seven speed settings, a forward/reverse switch and a built-in LED light to illuminate your work area. It comes with 30 interchangeable bits and a charging cable.
Best Prime Day tech deals
Amazon Echo Dot

The Echo Dot is a mini Bluetooth speaker with the Amazon Alexa voice assistant built-in. Use it to play your favorite music, podcasts, audiobooks and radio shows, as well as ask Alexa almost any question, including what the weather is in your area and how long your commute will be. The Echo Dot also lets you control compatible smart home devices and acts as a mesh Wi-Fi extender if you have an Eero network.
Blink Video Doorbell

See, hear and speak to visitors with Blink’s Video Doobell, which pairs with the brand’s app so you can livestream footage with a head-to-toe view from wherever you are. The app also sends you real-time notifications if you get a Blink subscription. The doorbell comes with three AA batteries inside, which you’ll need to replace about every two years, according to the brand. It’s compatible with Amazon-Alexa enabled devices, too.
Oura Ring 4

NBC Select reporter Harry Rabinowitz has tested the Oura Ring 4 for more than a year and says it’s a fantastic health, sleep and fitness tracker if you want something more subtle than a bulky smartwatch. Plus, he finds its data accurate. The Oura Ring gives you up to eight days of battery life per charge, and it’s water-resistant up to 328 feet. You’ll need an Oura Membership to see your data and use all of the app’s features — it’s $70 a year. Read our Oura Ring 4 review.
The Brick Phone Access Blocker

Five NBC Select staff members tested the Brick earlier this year, and each person says it helped them reduce their screen time, leading to better sleep, more focus at work and less of an urge to constantly scroll through social media. This little puck pairs with an app on your phone that you use to block specific apps, see timers and set modes for different parts of your day. Once you “Brick” your phone, the only way to “un-Brick” it is to tap it on the puck. Read our Brick review here.
Amazon Fire TV Stick HD

Once you plug the Amazon Fire Stick into your TV’s USB port, you can stream your favorite movies, shows and music across platforms. It also lets you play Xbox games without a separate console, control compatible smart home devices and connect a pair of Bluetooth headphones. This Fire Stick, which is optimized for HD TVs, comes with an Alexa Voice Remote.
Sony WH-1000XM5 Wireless Headphones

The Sony XM5 over-ear headphones have clear, crisp and detailed sound quality that make almost anything sound better. They also have great noise cancellation, call quality and battery life (up to 30 hours with noise canceling), and they can connect wirelessly or via a wire.
She’s Birdie Personal Safety Alarm

I’ve carried Birdie’s personal safety alarm on my keychain for years and I always gift it to loved ones who are college students or frequent travelers. To sound the alarm, you just pull the top ring, which also triggers a built-in strobe light to start flashing. The rechargeable alarm has an on/off switch so you don’t have to worry about it going off in your bag, as well as a built-in flashlight and charge indicator light. Read our Birdie Personal Safety Alarm review here.
Redtiger Golf Rangefinder

With six measurement modes, 7x magnification and a maximum range of 1200 yards, this versatile golf rangefinder is a must-have for every golfer. The device delivers reliable readings even in low visibility and cloudy conditions. It comes with a magnetic clip to attach to your belt, bag or cart and its small size makes it fit comfortably in your pocket.
Best Prime Day beauty deals
Grace & Stella Under Eye Patches Eye Glow Trio

I wear these under-eye patches every day, either in the morning to depuff and make me look more awake or at night to soothe and hydrate my skin after taking off my makeup. This set comes with 12 individually wrapped sets of Grace & Stella masks, so four of each type: moisturizing, energizing and illuminating. In my experience, the patches, which have a slight cooling effect, stick to my skin well, preventing them from sliding around while I have them on.
Laneige Lip Glowy Balm

Beyond giving you a sheer tint and shine, Laneige’s lightweight lip balm is moisturizing thanks to ingredients like murumuru and shea butters. Once I apply it, it keeps my lips hydrated and soft for hours. The tube is pocket-sized, so I always bring it with me everywhere, and I either wear it on its own or put it over a more pigmented, matte lipstick.
Kiehl’s Ultra Facial Cream

Kiehl’s Ultra Facial Cream (which dropped in price even more today) made our list of 100 best moisturizers and it’s beloved among NBC Select staff members across skin types. The lightweight, non-greasy cream is made with squalene and ceramides, hydrating ingredients that strengthen, soften and smooth out skin the barrier. It absorbs into skin quickly — I only need to wait a few seconds before layering makeup on top, and Kiehl’s says it can act like a primer with a satin finish.
Oral-B iO Series 3 Limited Electric Toothbrush

Oral-B’s electric toothbrush has features dentists told us to prioritize while shopping, including an automatic pressure sensor, a two-minute timer and soft bristles. You can choose from three cleaning settings, and its battery lasts for up to four weeks per charge, according to the brand. The toothbrush comes with a travel case and a charger.
Olaplex No. 7 Bonding Oil

I swear by Olaplex’s hair oil to soften, smooth out and add shine to my curls, and since a few drops go a long way, the bottle lasts me months. I always apply the oil before using hot tools since it offers heat protection up to 450 degrees Fahrenheit, and it gives you frizz control and humidity resistance for up to 72 hours, according to the brand. The oil, which you can use on all hair types, as well as wet and dry hair, is also color-safe and works on chemically treated hair.
Amika Perk Up Talc-Free Dry Shampoo

Hairstylists we talked to love Amika’s Perk Up Dry Shampoo because it blends in easily with an invisible finish, gives you volume and texture between washes, and isn’t too heavy on hair, which means it doesn’t make hair fall flat. They say you can use the dry shampoo from roots to ends. It’s made with rice starch to absorb moisture, oils and odors from the scalp, as well as nourishing sea buckthorn berry.
Biodance Bio-Collagen Real Deep Mask

This hydrating sheet mask is white when you apply it, but as your skin absorbs the serum it’s soaked in, it turns transparent. It’s formulated with collagen, hyaluronic acid and niacinamide to moisturize, help improve skin tone, texture and elasticity, as well as minimize the appearance of pores, fine lines and wrinkles, according to the brand. Biodance recommends leaving it on for three to four hours or overnight.
Best Prime Day home deals
Bissell Little Green Multi-Purpose Portable Carpet and Upholstery Cleaner

Every time I use my Bissell Little Green Machine to spot clean carpet, area rugs and upholstered furniture,  I’m amazed by how well it gets stains, odors and dirt out of fabric. It’s so easy to use: You just add water and the included cleaning solution to one removable tank, and then you use the nozzle to spray the solution on fabric, scrub the area and suction up the mess. The dirty water goes into a separate removable tank. The appliance comes with a self-cleaning tool to rinse out the hose when you’re done, a Tough Stain Tool and a Spraying Crevice Tool to get into tight spaces.
Jolie Filtered Showerhead

Now that I’ve used the Jolie Filtered Showerhead for more than three years, I can confidently say it’s made my hair softer and prevented my sensitive skin from constantly drying out. Its replaceable filter helps reduce the presence of chlorine, heavy metals and other contaminants in your water, according to the brand, and it comes with all the installation materials you’ll need. The showerhead fits on most standard U.S. shower pipes. Read our Jolie Filtered Showerhead review here.
Casper Sleep Original Pillow 2-Pack

The Casper Original Pillow is a NBC Select Bed & Bath Award winner, and years after testing it, many members of our team still sleep with it every night. The standard-size pillow comes in a pack of two, and it’s filled with a down-alternative material that gives it a medium-soft feel. It also has a breathable cotton percale cover and is fully machine-washable.
Blueair Blue Signature Small Smart Air Purifier

This air purifier’s HEPASilent filtration system removes pollutants from the air in your home and helps get rid of odors. There’s an air quality monitor built-in, and in auto mode, the air purifier changes its fan speed accordingly. You can adjust the appliance’s settings using the motion-activated touch display or via the Blueair app once you connect it via Wi-Fi. Blueair’s Signature Small air purifier is best for rooms up to 1,190 square-feet, according to the brand.
Best Prime Day kitchen deals
Le Creuset Nonstick Pro 8-inch Fry Pan

Le Creuset’s 8-inch nonstick pan is made from anodized aluminum, a material that helps it heat up quickly and distribute heat evenly, according to the brand. It also has a textured interior that helps you brown and sear food. The pan is compatible with all stovetops, oven-safe up to 500 degrees Fahrenheit, dishwasher-safe and works with metal utensils.
Owala FreeSip 40-Ounce Stainless Steel Water Bottle

I bring my Owala water bottle everywhere since it has a leakproof, push-lock lid, so I never worry about drinks spilling in my bag. There’s two ways to drink from it: by sipping through the hidden straw or tilting it back and chugging. The 40-ounce, dishwasher-safe bottle is made from insulated stainless steel to keep beverages cold for up to 24 hours, according to Owala, and you can screw off the cap to add ice or electrolyte powder through the wide opening. There’s also a built-in carry loop.
KitchenAid Ribbed Soft Silicone Oven Mitt (2-Pack)

These oven mitts are heat-resistant up to 500 degrees Fahrenheit to protect your hands from hot dishes, according to the brand. They have a ribbed, water-resistant silicone exterior, making them extra grippy and easy to wipe clean, as well as a soft interior lining for comfort and hanging loops for easy storage.
Ecolution Micro-Pop Popcorn Popper

Upgrade your microwaveable popcorn with this dishwasher-safe kitchen gadget. You use the popper’s lid to measure out your popcorn kernels before pouring them inside, and then you put it in the microwave for about three minutes. You can also put butter on top of the lid — it has little holes, so as the butter melts, it drips over the fresh popcorn. The 3-quart popper has a wide mouth, so you can use it to serve the snack, and its handle stays cool.
Stasher 4-Pack Starter Kit

I swapped disposable plastic bags for Stasher’s reusable, dishwasher-safe silicone ones years ago, and now I own too many to count. This pack comes with four in different sizes: snack, sandwich, stand-up mid and stand-up bowl. Each Stasher bag has a leak-free Pinch-Loc seal, and they’re microwave-, freezer-, sous-vide- and oven-safe up to 424 degrees Fahrenheit.Read our Stasher Bag review.
Best Prime Day heath and wellness deals
Liquid I.V. Hydration Multiplier Sugar-Free Electrolyte Powder

I mix Liquid IV’s electrolyte powder into my water before a long run and other outdoor activities in the heat. Each single-serve packet has 500 mg. of sodium and 370 mg. of potassium, which experts told us are the most important minerals to look for since we lose them at the highest rates while sweating. Liquid IV’s powder dissolves quickly if you give it a good stir, in my experience, and it comes in numerous sugar-free flavors, including Ring Pop Cherry, lemon lime and mandarin orange. (Be sure to speak to your doctor before taking any type of supplement.)
Orgain Organic Simple Vegan Protein Powder

Orgain has been my go-to protein powder for years — in fact, my Amazon order history says I’ve bought it over 20 times. Each serving has 20 grams of protein, and it’s made from vegan, soy-free and gluten-free ingredients, according to the brand. I add it to smoothies, oatmeal and baked goods, and beyond making them flavorful, it makes them extra creamy. You can buy Orgain’s Simple Plant Protein Powder in chocolate, vanilla and peanut butter. (Be sure to speak to your doctor before taking any type of supplement.)
Vital Proteins Collagen Peptides Powder Supplement (20 Individual Packets)

Each of these single-serve packets has 10 grams of unflavored collagen peptides inside, which you can dissolve into hot and cold liquids to drink. The collagen is sourced from grass-fed, pasture-raised bovine and it helps support the hair, skin, nails and joints, according to the brand — I’ve been taking it for years and notice the positive effects on my nails and hair. (Be sure to speak to your doctor before taking any type of supplement.)
Loop Quiet 2 Ear Plugs

NBC Select editor Mili Godio wears Loop’s Quiet Ear Plugs while sleeping to muffle noise on the busy city street outside her window. They’re not completely noise-canceling, so she’s still aware of her surroundings (and alarm clock), but they help her fall ( and stay) asleep. Godio says these ear plugs are comfortable (they come with ear tips in four sizes for a custom fit) and don’t fall out of her ears. You can reuse them for up to 5 years, according to the brand, and rinse them off before putting them inside the included carrying case for safe keeping. Read our Loop Ear Plugs review here.
Best Prime Day travel and outdoor deals
Bagsmart Toiletry Bag Travel Bag

Numerous NBC Select staff members rely on this toiletry bag to store all their essentials while traveling, and it’s so roomy that we often joke we can fit our entire bathroom inside it. It folds out into four clear panels with double-zipper pockets, some of which have interior elastic straps to keep bottles upright. The toiletry bag has a hook to hang it from when it’s unfolded, two carry straps to tote it around when it’s closed and an exterior front pocket.
The North Face Lichen Daypack

This backpack is endorsed by the American Chiropractic Association because it’s designed with features that support your back and shoulders, like a rounded back panel and shoulder straps. It has three separate compartments to store all your essentials, including a large main compartment with a 16-inch laptop sleeve. There’s also small organization pockets throughout the bag and two water bottle pockets on the outside.
LifeStraw Personal Water Filter

Take this personal water filter on hiking and camping trips, or pack it in your emergency kit. It removes bacteria, parasites, dirt and microplastics from water and lasts up to 1,000 gallons, according to the brand. Since the filter acts as a straw, you can drink directly from ponds, streams, lakes and more.
Bogg Bag Original Large Tote

“My Bogg Bag is the ultimate beach and pool companion. It easily fits everything needed for my three kids, and the waterproof design makes handling wet towels and clothes hassle-free,” says Becca Delman, a NBC senior production manager. “It’s even great for quick trips to the park, and spills, snacks, and messes wash away with ease.” The bag has a flat bottom, so you can sit it up on any surface, and it comes with two pouches you can connect to the holes on the inside or outside (Bogg sells additional accessories separately).
Yeti Hopper M-30 Soft Cooler

You can fit up to 26 cans and up to 36 pounds of ice inside this 7.2-gallon insulated soft cooler that has a leak-proof liner. Its wide mouth stays open to make filling it easy, and to close it, you simply push the sides in and let the built-in magnets create a tight seal. The bag has a shoulder strap and a variety of exterior attachment points to add other bags and accessories. (Note: This Prime Day price only applies to the black color.)

title: Father of 4 children found dead in NY apartment was days from seeing them following visitation battle
summary: A Utah father was days away from seeing his four children — the culmination of a years-long visitation battle — when he learned they had been killed in a heinous crime their grandmother is accused of committing.
story: A Utah father was days away from seeing his four children — the culmination of a years-long visitation battle — when he learned they had been killed in a heinous crime their grandmother is accused of committing.
Limited time: Save 25% on NBC News subscription
Get exclusive reporting, live Q&As and ad-free reading.

Brady Harmon said he was scheduled to pick up his children on Wednesday and return them to their mother in New York on Sept. 1, as part of a court-ordered parenting plan. Instead, Salt Lake City police delivered devastating news that his children — Harper Harmon, 13; Hudson Harmon, 11; and 10-year-old twins Gavin and Gracelynn Harmon — were found dead in a Mechanicville, New York, apartment.
The children’s mother, Sarah Myers, 44, and their maternal grandmother, Amy Steadman, 64, were also found dead, Mechanicville police said.

Officers were dispatched to the apartment after a neighbor reported not having seen Steadman for some time. Police Chief William Rabbitt said at a Thursday news conference that Steadman lived in the apartment, while Myers and the children lived separately within the same building.
“I don’t know what happened,” Harmon said in a phone interview Friday. “I have speculations, but not to be able to see my kids — that’s the part that sucks the most.”

Rabbitt said one of the children is believed to have suffered fatal sharp-force injuries. Evidence also points to intentional poisoning, including what he described as “numerous prescription and over-the-counter medications.” Causes and manners of death for all six victims remain pending further testing.
A handwritten note found in the apartment “strongly suggests that Amy Steadman was involved in the deaths,” Rabbitt said, declining to discuss the nature of the note.

Harmon said he learned of Steadman’s suspected role by watching the news conference. “I have no idea why they could think it would be her,” he said. “The handwritten note, that’s one thing, but she had nothing to lose or gain by my kids coming out here.”

Harmon said that he had been fighting for visitation rights for years. Court documents obtained by NBC News show the couple separated in November 2019,  with their divorce finalized the following year. After the separation, Myers relocated to New York with her mother and the children.

Under the custody agreement, Harmon was entitled to have the children from July through September, along with regular FaceTime calls. But he said Myers repeatedly blocked both. The last time he saw his children in person was in 2019, he said.
“There was always something that came up — she would find a way out of it,” he said. “Eventually, I had to get a different lawyer just to enforce the parenting plan.”
During the FaceTime calls he was permitted, Harmon said he could speak with all four children together for roughly 15 minutes with Myers present in the room.
He recalled small, ordinary moments: Gavin showing off things he had built, Gracelynn and Harper sharing drawings they’d made for him.

He said he never suspected that anyone would harm his children, and that in recent months Myers had seemed to be honoring the parenting plan more consistently. Separate court documents show that Myers was scheduled to appear in Utah court on Monday. Harmon said they were supposed to discuss the parenting agreement before he got his children.
“This is not something anyone could prepare for,” he said.

title: Utah weather conditions trigger historic red flag warning as wildfires rage in state
summary: The National Weather Service in Salt Lake City issued a red flag warning Friday morning as emergency workers continued to battle one of the state’s largest wildfires in its history.
story: The National Weather Service in Salt Lake City issued a red flag warning Friday morning as emergency workers continued to battle one of the state’s largest wildfires in its history.
Limited time: Save 25% on NBC News subscription
Get exclusive reporting, live Q&As and ad-free reading.
The red flag warning, issued when critical fire warnings are occurring or imminent, was to be in place through midnight Saturday.
“This is the FIRST Particularly Dangerous Situation Red Flag Warning issued in NWS Salt Lake City history. This is an exceptionally rare event,” the federal agency said in its warning.
A map of the area under the warning covered much of central and southwest Utah, with an area of the southwest, central and southern mountains also outlined as “particularly dangerous red flag.”

The particularly dangerous area includes the Cottonwood Fire, near the town of Beaver, which started Monday and had grown to covering almost nearly 71,000 acres by Thursday, 15 News reported. The fire forced evacuations.
The NWS warned that gusty winds and dry conditions would lead to rapid fire growth.
Utah also was dealing with the Iron Fire, which started June 19, and nearly destroyed the town of Eureka. The fire was about 27% contained Friday morning.

The fire danger led Utah Gov. Spencer Cox to issue executive order restricting fireworks statewide during the July 4 holiday, which marks the nation’s 250th birthday this year. The ban is in effect through July 5.
“Nothing about this decision was easy,” Cox said in a statement issued by his office Thursday.

“This is unlike anything we’ve seen in recent memory. We’re seeing fires spread farther and faster under conditions that defy historical expectations” Jamie Barnes, Utah state forester and director of the Utah Division of Forestry, Fire and State Lands, added in the statement.

Cox allowed cities and local communities to set aside areas where fireworks could be safely used. The city of Provo announced it would enforce a citywide prohibition on fireworks and would not designate a safe area for fireworks.
“This year is different,” Provo Mayor Marsha Judkins said in a statement. “The wildfire danger facing our community is real, and protecting lives, homes, and our natural spaces must come first.”

title: From The Sports Desk: The U.S. lost, but also saw return of star Christian Pulisic
summary: The United States lost last night, 3-2, on a late goal by Turkey. But the biggest development? Star Christian Pulisic returned from injury and showed he was healthy in time for the knockout stage.
story: The United States lost last night, 3-2, on a late goal by Turkey. But the biggest development? Star Christian Pulisic returned from injury and showed he was healthy in time for the knockout stage. The U.S. will take on Bosnia-Herzegovina in the round of 32.
Limited time: Save 25% on NBC News subscription
Get exclusive reporting, live Q&As and ad-free reading.
In action today, Norway’s goal-scoring giant Erling Haaland locks his Viking horns with France’s lighting-fast Kylian Mbappé, who is hunting down the World Cup scoring record.
There’s another slew of big group stage games today, so come follow our NBC News live blog.
World Cup Recap
The U.S. rested nine regular starters in its narrow 3-2 loss to Turkey, because the U.S. had already won its group. The game was decided by a scrappy goal in the final minute of stoppage time.
Coach Mauricio Pochettino was afforded the luxury of giving seven players their first minutes of the tournament.
“He didn’t have to change nine to 10 guys, but he does because he believes in us, and that’s what you want as a coach,” midfielder Brenden Aaronson said.

Now the team enters the do-or-die knockouts on July 1 in Santa Clara, California. The Bosnians finished third in Group B, behind Canada and Switzerland. At least the U.S. will have Pulisic back. He played against Turkey, returning to action for the first time since exiting the opening game with a calf injury.

The rest of yesterday’s action included Ecuador stunning four-time world champions Germany 2-1. Ivory Coast beat Curaçao 2-0, Japan and Sweden drew 1-1, Paraguay and Australia drew 0-0, and the Netherlands finished top of its group by beating Tunisia 3-1.
Attendance for this summer’s World Cup has already surpassed the record 3,587,538 set by the 1994 World Cup, which was hosted in the U.S., according to FIFA.
World Cup Preview
Shortly after beating Senegal, Norwegian superstar Haaland said he didn’t care about Norway’s final Group I game against France because his team had already qualified for the knockouts.
But the rest of the world most certainly does, as the Manchester City forward will face off against legendary French striker Mbappé.
The European goal machines — who both command a weekly salary north of $600,000 — have started the tournament with a bang.
Mbappé has scored four goals in two games to push his World Cup total to 16, two behind Lionel Messi’s record for most all time, while Haaland has scored twice each in wins over Senegal and Iraq.

Meanwhile, Seattle’s stadium is expected to be brimming with rainbow flags today for a World Cup match between teams representing two of the most repressive countries for lesbian, gay, bisexual and transgender people.

It was just a coincidence that the city’s “Pride Match” ended up as a high-stakes matchup between Iran and Egypt — with advancement from Group G still up in the air — and the move has come with plenty of pushback from both countries.
What We’re Watching
World Cup! All the games are on Telemundo and Peacock, and all times are Eastern:
3 p.m.: Norway vs. France3 p.m.: Senegal vs. Iraq8 p.m.: Uruguay vs. Spain8 p.m.: Cape Verde vs. Saudi Arabia11 p.m.: New Zealand vs. Belgium11 p.m.: Egypt vs. Iran
3 p.m.: Norway vs. France
3 p.m.: Senegal vs. Iraq
8 p.m.: Uruguay vs. Spain
8 p.m.: Cape Verde vs. Saudi Arabia
11 p.m.: New Zealand vs. Belgium
11 p.m.: Egypt vs. Iran
That’s it for now! We’ll be back Monday.

title: ‘Love Island USA’s’ Sol Dean Reveals the Couple She’s ‘Glad to Be Wrong’ About
summary: “Love Island USA” Season 8 bombshell Sol Mýa Dean’s time in Fiji was short but tumultuous — entering the villa on Day 7 and being dumped on Day 15.
story: “Love Island USA” Season 8 bombshell Sol Mýa Dean’s time in Fiji was short but tumultuous — entering the villa on Day 7 and being dumped on Day 15.
The Southern California-native quickly formed a connection with Sincere Rhea upon entering the villa and coupled up with him on Day 10, per America’s vote.
Their connection was cut short just four episodes later, when Sincere chose to rekindle his relationship with Melanie Moreno during the “You’ve Got Mail” challenge.

The shake-up left Sol coupled up with fellow bombshell Caleb McDaniel by default, but the pair only spent one day together before being voted among the least popular couples. That night, Sol was selected by a group of male islanders to be sent home alongside Gabriel Vasconcelos, who entered the villa as a bombshell on Day 2.
Upon hearing the news of the vote, Sol told the islanders that no one seemed open to getting to know her when she arrived, and she thanked the cast for putting her out of her “misery.”
Rather than shedding tears on her way out the door, she paused a final time to tell the islanders to make sure future newcomers don’t feel “excluded” like she did in the villa.
Below, Sol shares more about what really went down — from the emotional pie challenge to Sincere’s speech at her dumping — and which islander she thinks should win it all.
This interview has been lightly edited for clarity.

I think it was a lot of stuff. I don’t know, it was just rough going in there, it was a lot. There was a lot that was just happening, and all I’m gonna say is that it was more than two. But it was just emotional, like hearing them pick me for answers that they didn’t really get to know me for. I feel like that was my big thing. I just came in, they knew my name is Sol, and I’m a bombshell, and nothing else, so I just kind of felt a little dissed where I’m like, where are these warranted when no one’s gotten to know me?

Maybe applying more pressure with the guys. But I also want things to feel mutual, and if they’re happy and content where they’re at, then I’m not going to overly want to pursue something. So it was hard, kind of finding the silver lining of what I should do and what I shouldn’t do, but I felt like (with) the experience I did the best with the cards I was dealt.
I don’t know if he wanted to get back for me humbling him, but I was just like, OK, this is like the finale cherry on the top of you being able to just kind of dump me.

I don’t know, I didn’t feel like the situation was fair, honestly, and him saying that I wasn’t in a progressive couple when he knows that I obviously put most of my time and energy into getting to know him, because what he was telling me versus what he was saying to other people was different. But me, I’m someone that likes to take things at face value, and I mean, I won’t really have doubt until there’s a reason, so I mean, I was feeling like a bunch of different ways.
At least, I don’t know, someone else could have said it.

I just feel like I bit my tongue a lot, honestly, to keep things cordial and to have peace for living with these people 24/7. It’s also being filmed, so you’re kind of trying to navigate things in the best way, and I don’t really know if they knew like the effect that they were doing to me in the villa. And I feel like that’s just more so something I want to touch on when they do come out, and how they made that not a wonderful experience for me … There’s no shade. I hope a lot of them are still in their couples, but do I want to explain how they were making me feel while my time in the villa was very short and present there? I do want to kind of address that with them. So, that’s mainly my focus for that.

The hate on Trinity is a super misconception. On “Aftersun,” I feel like a lot of people kind of misconstrued my words, but in the beginning I said I wanted to get to know Trinity more because she was hilarious. She’s funny as hell. I felt like after watching some of the episodes, too, it just kind of made me validate the feelings I was feeling in the villa about what was being said and why there was just like, random tension. But again, I feel like the misconception would be Trinity and what I said on “Aftersun.”

I only said what I said on “Aftersun” because Bryce did pull Kayda multiple times and get rejected. It’s just kind of like, in my mind, if I’m keeping it plain simple at face value of what I’m seeing, if you were happy with your couple, you wouldn’t want to keep exploring, so that was just kind of more my premise on that. I didn’t say I think that she’s prettier than her. I could have obviously delivered it better, and I feel like that’s something I need to work on ... There’s no hate on Trinity. I think she’s amazing, and honestly, out of everyone, I think she should win this, so I’ll stand on that.

I haven’t really watched Casa Amor, I only watched like the first episode, but like, the little clips I have been seeing, I just saw both of them kind of like crying, I believe for each other, and I’m glad I’m wrong.

When you’re in the villa, like, a day feels like a month, and I already know that they had a connection brewing, and I wasn’t saying that they didn’t have a connection brewing, it was more so, like, Bryce just kept pulling Kayda and got rejected, so it was like, I didn’t say that they didn’t have a connection. But I’m honestly glad, I’m glad to even see this pan out. I think it’s great. I’m glad to be wrong on that, I do feel like they have a connection.

I feel like it’ll make the girls just more open, if anything, and I feel like that was maybe the point. The girls are super locked in and just super forgiving with their guys, but if you got double the men, and you just saw how your current person was moving, like it’s, “Go do your big one, girl,” you know. Go find someone else in Casa and just give other people a fair shot. So I hope that they’re doing that, honestly.

I kind of knew the boys were gonna go crazy in Casa, just since they’re not being watched. So, I don’t know if I could boil it down to just one person. I couldn’t really answer that, but I feel like all of them kind of just felt like the chains were being broken loose. It was like a brand new show happening again, having a bunch of people get introduced, you get to know everyone without, like, a current partner watching, or like reprimanding you for it. I don’t know, I feel like they all were kind of, like, really excited from what I saw.

Watching it from a viewer’s perspective is interesting. It’s a little cringe, but it’s also interesting because I’m like, completely myself all the time, and I feel like, again, you guys get one hour out of 24. Watching it back is kind of just like, there’s a lot of things that just were happening that I wasn’t aware of, so maybe asking like questions more, I feel like, again, I take a lot of things at face value, so maybe kind of trying to get the scoop, and what’s going on. Why is blank upset, or whatever it is. It’s interesting watching yourself back.

title: Melanie Martinez Reveals Name of Next Album
summary: Melanie Martinez has revealed exclusively on TODAY the name of her upcoming album will be “Elysium.”
story: Melanie Martinez has already revealed the name of her next album, just three months after the release of “Hades.”
The former “Voice” contestant dropped the news exclusively on TODAY June 26, saying her 2026 album, “Hades” is Part 1 of a two-album effort.
“There’s another album that I haven’t said the name yet, but maybe I’ll say it now. Why not?” the “Dollhouse” singer said as the crowd on the plaza went wild.
“The second album is called ‘Elysium.’ That’s all I’ll say, but it’s a utopian reference, so it’s the other part. It’s the counterpart to it,” she added, as the crowd continued howling.
While she didn’t reveal too much information about the album itself, she did talk about how “Hades,” which topped the Billboard rock and alternative albums chart in April, came together.

“With ‘Hades,’ it was just kind of reflecting on the current times, and the world feels very dystopian, so I wanted to make a dystopian record,” she said, noting she was inspired by the book “Parable of the Sower” by Octavia E. Butler.
“I thought it was time to make an album about all of the topics that I felt were really kind of heavy on my heart and I’m just really happy that resonate with it,” she said.
Martinez has quite the loyal fanbase, which has grown since she appeared on “The Voice” in 2012. It’s a rise that has surprised her.
“I’m always in awe, and unbelievably grateful,” she said.

“And I just feel lucky that I get to do what I love and that I get to put on shows and create safe spaces for people all over the world to come together who are like-minded and creative and passionate,” she added. “And it’s inspiring and I just want to do it for the rest of my life, so I’m just really grateful.”

title: ‘Love Island USA’ Removes Another Contestant, Alannah Keyser, For Using Racial Slur
summary: “Love Island USA” contestant Alannah Keyser will no longer appear on the program after a video of her using a racial slur surfaced.
story: “Love Island USA” contestant Alannah Keyser, who joined the show on the June 21 episode of the hit Peacock series, will no longer appear on the program following the June 25 episode, the streaming service has confirmed to TODAY.com.
Her removal comes after a video resurfaced in which she used a racial slur while singing the lyrics to the 2019 Roddy Rich song “The Box.”
Keyser, 21, hails from Miami and had served as one of the Casa Armor bombshells. She appeared to have made a connection with Zach Georgiou.

“Love Island USA” has removed other cast members before. Vasana Montgomery was among the islanders who appeared in an introduction video for Season 8 on May 28. Clips of her using a racial slur surfaced shortly after that and she was dismissed from the cast the next day. She did not appear on the June 2 premiere.

A pair of Season 7 islanders were also removed from the villa in 2025. Yulissa Escobar appeared in one episode before viewers were informed that she was no longer on the show after clips of her using racial slurs on a podcast came to light.

“In those clips, I used a word I never should’ve used, a racial slur,” she later wrote, in part, on Instagram. “I used it ignorantly, not fully understanding the weight, history, or pain behind it. I wasn’t trying to be offensive or harmful, but I recognize now that intention doesn’t excuse impact. And the impact of that word is real. It’s tied to generations of trauma, and it is not mine to use.”
Cierra Ortega, who was also on Season 7, left the show toward the end of that season after videos of her using racial slurs surfaced.
She later apologized in a TikTok video.

“I completely agree with the network’s decision to remove me from the villa. I think that this is something that deserved punishment, and the punishment is absolutely been received, for sure,” she said.
Escobar and Ortega departed the show under similar circumstances, which Escobar noted.
“I’m not justifying what I did, I know what I did and what I said was wrong, and I know what Cierra said was wrong and I know it hurt communities,” she said about Ortega in a TikTok video.

title: ‘Love Island USA’s’ Gabriel Reacts to His Elimination, Reveals Where He Stands With Beatriz
summary: The girls of “Love Island USA” said they chose to dump Gabriel Vasconcelos from the villa because “he’s constantly looking for the next best thing, and we don’t feel like the villa will ever bring him someone he’s content with.”Gabriel says he’s still asking why
story: The girls of “Love Island USA” said they chose to dump Gabriel Vasconcelos from the villa because “he’s constantly looking for the next best thing, and we don’t feel like the villa will ever bring him someone he’s content with.”
Gabriel says he’s still asking why.

The bombshell entered the villa on Day 2 and quickly formed connections with multiple islanders. He first coupled up with Beatriz Hatz before viewers voted to pair him with bombshell Jen Terry, a connection Gabriel says he was still growing when his journey came to an end.

After viewers voted again, this time for their favorite couple, Gabriel and Jen were left vulnerable. The boys safe from the vote chose to send Sol Mýa Dean home, arguing she was the only girl at risk of elimination not in a strong couple, sparing Jen.
But instead of Sol’s unattached counterpart, Caleb McDaniel, the girls chose to send Gabriel home, claiming he hadn’t fully committed to one relationship and would likely not settle.

In a conversation with TODAY.com, Gabriel opens up about his reaction to the reasoning behind his elimination, the criticism he received for exploring multiple connections and the moments viewers didn’t get to see during his time in Fiji.
This interview has been lightly edited for clarity.

I’m still asking myself, “Why?” Is it because I was exploring? Is it because I was doing the “Love Island” experience? I was the “craziest one,” you know? I don’t see any point about that answer. I’m sorry, but I’m completely honest and upfront with my answer, so everyone knows that ... I just had to accept that, the fact that I was leaving.

I mean, he’s right. He’s right. We have different backgrounds, like, it doesn’t mean that I’m ... lustful, you know. Like Brazilians, we are different, and I was literally having a good time with the girls that I was with, nobody was complaining about it.

Not even Jen was understanding the fact that I was leaving. She was like, oh, s---, like, I don’t believe that. Because it wasn’t making sense. But I’m so glad that the guys were defending me because they knew about how I am, how I was treating the girls.

In this case, like, Jen ... they know that my connection with Jen was growing. And yeah, like me and her know about it. That’s why I’m not worried about anything else, and I know that what I did and that was right.

All that I have to say about it, like, all the Latin language, they’re more romantic. So when I’m gonna talk about something specific, especially for feelings, I can be more — I can give more details of how I’m feeling. Not that I’m gonna express perfect in Portuguese, but just differently, you know? And I can give more. I can go over more about it, and that’s how I feel.

I feel like English, I also can do it, but it’s not the same way. It’s not the same vibe. The thing is, if I hear, “I love you” from someone, I know that people are gonna be loving me, but I feel if I hear “eu te amo,” which means “love you” in Portuguese or in Spanish, it means more for me.

But that’s one thing that it’s hard to explain for everyone because you just feel it. Like, if I hear “eu te amo” from my mom, it’s different than if I hear “love you” — I’m gonna think that she’s lying to me, because, you know, that’s just something different, it’s hard to explain.

I think it was the fact that most of my conversations with the guys and with the girls were not shown. You know, it’s literally 24 hours in one hour, a lot of people talking, and if they decided they just had to put a few moments of me like, making out, that was it.

I don’t know, I mean, I think I was flirting more than everyone else, and they only got those moments. People saw me talking with Sol (for) like 5 seconds, then we went to the bed together, then I started kissing her. No, no, no! We had a conversation at least for 30 minutes before. We were talking in front of the pool for, I don’t know, like 15 minutes and then we went upstairs. We started talking like in the chairs in front of each other for 15 minutes and then we went to the bed.
So like, nobody’s seen that, and it was looking like, oh, I went upstairs with her, I say, oh, I know what I want, and “Boom.” No, but it wasn’t that, and that’s funny.
We were watching it together, like, “Oh s---, that’s crazy.”

I think both of my connections, they are not showing most of our things. Because we were talking the whole day, not specific when we’re going to chat. When I was pulling them for a chat, OK, I was pulling them, but during the day, we were talking about a lot of things. I feel like we lost at least 90% of conversation.

Because if I was being just crazy, just making out, probably they wouldn’t be crying for me. People need to think about it, like, “Why they were crying for Gabriel?” or, “Why were they feeling sad?” or something. Because they had something else, they had some different feelings, because we were having a good time literally. But that’s one thing that I just, I was like, as we were leaving the villa, we know that. That’s life.
Yeah, we talked. We talked two days ago. Yeah, we are good.
No. No, I don’t have any regrets about anything.

I  really appreciate everyone who is changing their opinion about me because they’re already noticing the way that I am. When I had the chance to talk on “Aftersun,” they were like, “Oh s---, like he knows how to express himself,” you know? And I like that because I am like that. I was one of the guys who was most like making jokes with everyone and talking s---. I was always like present with everyone.

That’s why I was like, “Oh, s---, I’m not — where’s myself in this episode?” But I’m good with myself, that’s the most important thing. I know what I’ve done, and in the villa, everyone knows how I was treating them, and yeah, I was literally being myself and that’s the most important for me.
Mm, good question. Everyone was asking me the same thing. Oh, guys. “Oh, Gabriel, we are missing you in Casa Amor.” This and that. But, guys, I’m not there. I’m at my casa!

title: ‘X-Men’ Star Tyler Mane Opens Up About Breast Cancer Diagnosis: ‘Check Yourselves, Men’
summary: Tyler Mane reveals how he was diagnosed with male breast cancer. The “X-Men” actor and wrestler shares his symptom, treatment and risk factor for the disease.
story: Tyler Mane never imagined the hard lump that appeared on the left side of his chest would turn out to be breast cancer.

The “X-Men” actor and former wrestler says he first noticed it last fall, but wasn’t overly concerned. He thought it would go away. It was his wife, Renae, who urged him to get the lump checked out and removed this spring.
The diagnosis: invasive ductal carcinoma.
It was caught early, at Stage 1, when it hadn’t yet spread. Genetic testing revealed Mane has a gene mutation that increases his risk of breast cancer and other types, too.
“It never even crossed my mind that I would be having male breast cancer,” Mane, 61, who lives in Atlanta, tells TODAY.com in a digital exclusive interview.
“I was being conscious of heart conditions, but never breast cancer… I really want to say, ‘Check yourselves, men.’”
He announced his diagnosis on Instagram this month, calling it “super rare.”
Fewer than 1% of breast cancer cases occur in men, according to the American Cancer Society. Mane is one of 2,670 men expected to be diagnosed with the disease in the U.S. in 2026.
For men, the average lifetime risk of getting breast cancer is about 1 in 755, compared to 1 in 8 for women.
Mane going public “is a valuable reminder that men can get breast cancer,” Dr. Harold Burstein, director of breast cancer education at the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute, told NBC News.
For many men, “it’s just not widely known that it’s a possibility,” Burstein added.
How Mane Discovered the Breast Cancer
Mane says his first reaction was to keep his diagnosis secret, calling it “kind of embarrassing.”
He explains he’s always been self-conscious about his chest.
“It’s been an issue for me because that’s where I carry my fat,” Mane says.
“All the way through high school, I had a fattier chest, meatier chest, and some kids can be cruel and make fun of you.”

When the former wrestler started a health journey about two years ago, he had a DEXA scan to measure his body fat percentage and was shocked to discover it came in at 33%. Mane then started working out to get back into shape, and it dropped to 16% last fall. That’s when he began noticing the lump in his left breast, close to his nipple.
“That was pretty much the only sign that I had,” he says.
“(But) as a guy, you just put stuff off. You go, ‘I’ll get better, I’ll be fine. Don’t worry about it, it’ll go away.’”
In the spring, Mane’s brother died of a massive heart attack. Health was on the family’s mind, and Mane’s wife insisted he have a doctor look at the lump.
His primary care physician “kind of dismissed it,” but said he could go to a dermatologist, Mane recalls. The dermatologist sent him to a surgeon, who removed the lump on April 1.
The biopsy results revealed it was invasive ductal carcinoma, the most common type of breast cancer in men, according to the National Library of Medicine.
“That’s when my life changed,” Mane says.
Genetic Testing Reveals Risk Factor
He underwent a PET scan and had three lymph nodes removed from his left armpit. All came back clear, confirming it was Stage 1 cancer. No more breast tissue needed to be removed.

Genetic testing revealed that Mane has the BRCA2 mutation, which increases a person’s risk of developing breast cancer and other cancers. “I am definitely a mutant,” he jokes, adding that it’s important information for his two children.

Other testing showed Mane’s removed tumor was fast-growing, so he started four rounds of chemotherapy to make sure no cancer cells would be left behind. His first session was on June 8, and he has three weeks in between the treatments.
The former wrestler does squats and pushups to keep up his strength.

“Right now, I’m feeling fine, but it has been a roller coaster,” he says, noting his facial hair is starting to fall out. “I was very tired, resting all the time, but you have to keep a strong mindset to come through this.”
Mane takes an estrogen blocker since his cancer was hormone receptor (HR) positive, which means it grows in response to estrogen.
He’ll undergo radiation once the chemotherapy is finished.
Mission to Raise Awareness

Mane is grateful to all the fans who’ve reached out to support and encourage him. His next project is “Mane AF,” a podcast he plans to do with his wife that focuses on “dealing with midlife mayhem and marriage,” he says. It’s launching in about a month.

But his big mission is to raise awareness about breast cancer in men. Men have reached out to him on social media saying they wish they had gotten checked earlier, before their disease reached Stage 3 or 4.

Men are more likely than women to die after being diagnosed with breast cancer, studies have found. One reason is that more men are diagnosed once the disease has already spread, which is likely due to a lack of awareness about breast cancer in men and screening for it, researchers noted.
Mane wants other men to know they’re not alone. He’s no longer embarrassed about his diagnosis.
“Becoming an advocate for it is something that I felt that I had to do,” he says.
“There’s no use hiding from stuff like that, because our bodies are our bodies, and we’re designed the way we are, and we have one go ‘round in life and make the best of it.”

title: ‘Voicemails For Isabelle’ Is a Netflix Hit. Here’s What Critics Are Saying
summary: What critics are saying about the new from-com “Voicemails for Isabelle” which is an audience hit.
story: “Voicemails for Isabelle,” which dropped on Netflix in June, is a rom-com with depth. The movie earns some real laughs, all while hitting some deep commentary on grief, sisterhood and the barriers within digital connection.
The film follows a pastry chef, Jill (Zoey Deutch), who, after the loss of her sister, Isabelle (Ciara Bravo), turns to her voicemail box to process her emotions.
Small problem: Isabelle’s number has been reassigned to someone else. Enter Wes (Nick Robinson), a real estate agent who is on the market to learn more about the mysterious voice spamming his phone.
With a 91% Rotten Tomatoes audience score, viewers seem to be walking away from “Voicemails for Isabelle” feeling very satisfied by this Deutch and Robinson team-up.
But what are the critics saying? Read below to find out.
The New York Times

With her lead role alongside Glen Powell from the 2018 film “Set It Up,” Deutch is no stranger to the rom-com genre. Writing for the New York Times, Glenn Kenney highlights her performance as a standout.
“The appealing Zoey Deutch is the best reason to watch “Voicemails for Isabelle,” he writes.

As Wes’ interest in learning more about Jill builds up, he flies from Texas to California to not-so sneakily run into her. Kenney draws comparisons between Wes’ efforts and rom-com inspirations the writers possibly drew from.
“Wes does other deeds reminiscent of the 1999 article in The Onion: ‘Romantic-Comedy Behavior Gets Real-Life Man Arrested,’ he writes.
Kenney ultimately lands on the film being a bit too predicable.
He writes, “One watches the blossoming romance between Wes and Jill with, of course, a certain dread about the inevitable shoe dropping.”
Deadline
Chemistry saves the day in the review by Pete Hammond, chief film critic for Deadline.

Although he finds Netflix “sometimes getting it right, sometimes getting it wrong” with this film, and similar to Kenney, faults its predictability, Hammond says the movie ”rises above the familiar with the sheer delight of the chemistry between its two stars.”
Hammond identifies the film’s rollercoaster ride of emotions quite simply: “You’ll laugh. You’ll cry. All that stuff.”
“Voicemails for Isabelle” rises above for Hammond through “the couple at the center of it all.”

He especially praises Deutch’s performance, saying it “works on every level” and calling her “a knockout that qualifies her a spot in the rom-com hall of fame alongside Julia Roberts, Kate Hudson and Meg Ryan.”

But wait — Hammond didn’t forget about her male co-star. “Robinson also has great appeal, perfectly balancing a role that might come across as more cynical in other hands, but he nails it,” he writes.
“Together they are winning.”
The Wrap
The Wrap concludes that this popular new film is only “an enthusiastic but misguided romance that only sometimes lives up to its references,” according to William Bibbiani.
While he believes the film “doesn’t want us to think Wes is a creep,” Bibbiani has some strong words for Wes’ character or any fans of his.
“If Wes got what was coming to him every time he did something creepy he’d be an unrecognizable bruise in the shape of a human by the end of this movie,” he writes.
Even as the film progresses, this critic cannot escape his Wes hatred.

“To Robinson’s credit he almost makes the character work, although it takes 90% of the movie to get there,” he writes. “The conclusion, bolstered by a laugh out loud Taylor Swift joke, finds Wes defeated and humbled.”
Decider
Jesse Hassenger awards the female lead as “the rom-com queen of 2026” with this new film.
With “Voicemails For Isabelle,” this critic thinks Deutch has found her sweet spot: “characters who are chipper, even mischievous, with the heart of a classic romantic heroine.”
“She’s just synthetic enough to power through a Netflix rom-com with superhuman energy, and just sincere enough to make you care about it,” Hassenger continues.

title: Are Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce Really Getting Married at MSG? What a New Permit Reveals
summary: New clues point to Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce getting married sometime over Independence Day weekend at New York City’s Madison Square Garden.
story: She’s known for her “Love Story,” but right now Taylor Swift’s romance with Travis Kelce is shrouded in mystery amid growing rumors about their wedding.

Speculation over the possibility that the couple, who announced their engagement in August 2025 following a high profile courtship, will get married around Independence Day reached a fever pitch after a permit was filed with New York City that suggests a major event is set to occur that weekend at Madison Square Garden.

A source at Madison Square Garden who declined to be identified  to discuss a confidential matter tells NBC News that the arena has blocked off July 2 through July 4 while planning for an event, although it is not clear if the event in question is for the Swift-Kelce nuptials.
Representatives for Swift and Kelce did not immediately respond to NBC News’ requests for comment.
What To Know About the Madison Square Garden Permit

Leading to the chatter that the wedding will occur at Madison Square Garden — known as “The World’s Most Famous Arena” — is the fact that a permit has been filed, as first reported by The New York Times.

Dora Pekec, a spokesperson for the New York City Mayor’s Office, confirmed the permit’s existence and its details as reported by the Times, including a requested crowd of somewhere between 500 and 999 people, as well as street closures around the arena between July 2 and July 4. There is also a request to put up a tent outside. Representatives for Madison Square Garden did not immediately respond to NBC News’ request for comment.

Event planning company Winick Productions, which has helped put on major red carpets, including ones at Madison Square Garden, filed the application with New York City’s Street Activity Permit Office in early June, according to The New York Times. Winick Productions’ president, Elliot Winick, declined to comment to NBC News.
Pekec did not share a copy of the permit or confirm to NBC News who filed it.
New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani previously alluded to Swift and Kelce’s potential wedding during a press conference on June 15 when discussing the city’s ability to oversee big events.

“We are the biggest city in the country,” Mamdani said. “We are used to big events, and we are incredibly excited for this one. We know it coincides with the Knicks’ Finals run. We know it coincides with July 4, America 250, Taylor Swift’s wedding — all happening at the same time — and we are so excited to welcome the world here.”
Does Anyone Actually Know When or Where the Wedding Will Be?
San Francisco 49ers tight end George Kittle and his wife, Claire, say they were invited to the wedding and will attend, although they don’t know where it will be held.
“I have no idea,” he told “Entertainment Tonight” on June 24 in a joint interview with his wife.
“We don’t know,” she added.
“I actually asked Travis last night, and he laughed at me,” he continued.
“I’m half expecting there just to be a jumbo jet on a runway and they put us on a plane somewhere,” he added. “I don’t have expectations. I know it’s going to be amazing.”
Who Else Will Attend the Wedding?
Suki Waterhouse, who has opened for Swift in concert, has confirmed she plans to be on hand.

“Nothing scarier than the actual planning part, right?” Waterhouse told Variety in May, referring to her possible nuptials with fiancé Robert Pattinson. “I’m gonna go to Taylor’s wedding, and maybe I’ll get some inspiration. It will be amazing.”
Jack Antonoff, a longtime collaborator and friend of Swift, was a little more coy about the matter when he appeared with his band, Bleachers, on TODAY May 22.
“Are you going to any big weddings this summer?” Craig Melvin asked, without mentioning Swift and Kelce by name.
“I hope. Yeah, I hope so, man,” he said. “I love being married. I’m happy you love it too.”
“That’s all you’re gonna give me, huh?” Craig playfully replied.
What Is Swift’s Connection to Madison Square Garden?
Swift has performed multiple concerts at the arena, with her first occurring during a talent show at a Knicks game in 2003, when she was 13.

She also sat courtside during Game 4 of the NBA Finals earlier this month, witnessing the Knicks stage the largest comeback in Finals history to go up 3-1 over the San Antonio Spurs, before winning the NBA title the next game.

title: Casey McQuiston Unveils Cover of ‘Red, White & Royal Blue’ Sequel Novella
summary: See the cover of Casey McQuiston’s “Red, White & Royal Blue: The Private Correspondence,” and find out what it’s about and how it’s related to the first book and movie.
story: Casey McQuiston will continue the story of the star-crossed romance between the first son of the United States and a prince of England with the novella, “Red, White & Royal Blue: The Private Correspondence,” and TODAY.com has an exclusive first look at the cover.

“The Private Correspondence,” out Dec. 1, will contain notes and letters between Alex Claremont-Diaz and Prince Henry from the day they went public with their relationship at the end of 2019’s “Red, White & Royal Blue” to 20 years later.

The epistolary novella’s cover is pink with sprayed edges and red and blue floral details. McQuiston tells TODAY.com in a statement it also contains an Easter egg; the florals on the cover are inspired by Alex and Henry’s wedding flowers.

“I could not be more in love with this cover,” McQuiston said in a statement. “We wanted to capture the handmade ephemeral vibe of a personal collection, with bits of playfully timeless Texan and English aesthetics and nods to a long and flourishing life together, all inspired by Alex and Henry’s wedding flowers.”

Fans of the “Red, White & Royal Blue” will remember that a major plot point of the book and its 2023 film adaptation is that Alex and Henry’s private emails were leaked, revealing their secret, trans-Atlantic relationship. The premise of “The Private Correspondence” establishes that “they vowed never to use email again for their personal communication,” instead relying on “handwritten love letters.”
The novella will include the characters’ personal letters, post cards and journal entries, revealing “sarcastic bon mots to sexy fantasies to emotional discussions about starting a family.”
In addition to “The Private Correspondence,” the “Red, White & Royal Blue” is getting another addition to its film universe.

In October 2025, Amazon MGM Studios revealed the title for the film’s sequel, “Red, White & Royal Wedding,” with a screenplay written by McQuiston, Gemma Burgess and Matthew Lopez. The movie entered production in January.
On their Instagram, McQuiston clarified that “The Private Correspondence” is “totally unrelated to the film sequel. It is a continuation of the book.”
They added that the novella is “spoiler-free” relative to the upcoming film sequel, adding that the film world “is slightly different from the book, and so things unfold in different ways.”

“Most of the things in (‘The Private Correspondence’) have been inside my head for years and years as the futures I envisioned for A&H,” they wrote on Instagram. “It has been so strange and surprising and joyful to come back to them a full decade after I first met them, to reconnect with the A&H who live on the page, and to have grown alongside them over the past 10 years of my own life.”

title: Romance Author Kennedy Ryan Shares the Meaningful Story Behind Her Pen Name
summary: Kennedy Ryan isn’t the author’s real name. The romance author shares the story behind her pen name and why she chooses to use it.
story: While readers may know the intimate details of romance author Kennedy Ryan’s characters, they may be less familiar with one major detail about the novelist herself: her real name.
During the June 25 episode of “Open Book with Jenna,” Ryan told Jenna Bush Hager about the meaningful backstory of her pen name, and why she had to pick one out in the first place.
After she got her first book deal, Ryan had to sit down with the HR department of the job she had at the time.
“They were like, ‘That’s great. You’re going to have to write under a pseudonym, because whatever you’re writing can’t be associated with us,” she remembers.
That human resources policy ended up benefitting her in more ways than one.
“I’m kind of glad. I like a little bit of space, I like a little distance,” she added. “I share my mental health, I share about everything.”
She’d picked out the name Kennedy Ryan long before she started writing — but it was originally for a different reason.
“I had always wanted a daughter and we had my son, and then a lot happened,” she told Jenna. “I ended up not having another child, but I always wanted to (use the) name ‘Kennedy Ryan.’”
Even as more of the world comes to know her as as bestselling author and advocate Kennedy Ryan, a few important people still stick with her real name, she told Jenna.
“Does your husband call you Kennedy?” Jenna asked.
“He’s been practicing,” Ryan replied. “Of course, at home, he’s not calling me Kennedy.”
But, she added, as she ramps up for upcoming book events, she prepares him to stick to the pseudonym.
Ryan’s latest book, “Score,” is a second chance romance and the second installment in her Hollywood Renaissance series.
With her romances, she told Jenna she wants women to realize there’s someone out there “you deserve.”
“Culture tells you the bar is way down here, and that no one’s going to clear it. Settle. I’m like, ‘Raise the bar, babe,’” she said.

title: Hallmark and Chicago Bears Will Team Up for Next ‘Holiday Touchdown’ Movie This Christmas
summary: Da Bears are ready to bring da Christmas cheer on the Hallmark Channel.
story: Da Bears are ready to bring da Christmas cheer on the Hallmark Channel.

Hallmark Media, the NFL and Skydance Sports announced in a TODAY exclusive on June 25 that they are teaming up to produce “Holiday Touchdown: A Bears Love Story,” following the success of Kansas City Chiefs and Buffalo Bills-themed Christmas movies the past two years.
The premiere date has not yet been set, but the new movie will come out later in 2026 as part of Hallmark Channel’s 17th annual Countdown to Christmas films.
Bears head coach Ben Johnson and second-year tight end Colston Loveland announced the new film in a video released on June 25.

The Bears-themed holiday movie follows “Holiday Touchdown: A Bills Love Story,” which was released in 2025. The first film of the series, “Holiday Touchdown: A Chiefs Love Story,” was released in 2024 and featured a cameo from TODAY’s Jenna Bush Hager.
Both of the first two films ranked among the top cable movies of the year in total viewers, according to Nielsen.

“After the tremendous success of our first two Holiday Touchdown movies, we knew we wanted to continue the tradition by partnering with another iconic NFL franchise and fan base. The Chicago Bears have a rich history, passionate supporters, and a culture that extends far beyond the footballfield, making them a perfect fit for a heartwarming holiday story,” Hallmark Media President Darren Abbott said in a news release.

One of the NFL’s oldest and most storied franchises, the Bears have been a team on the rise under Johnson and star quarterback Caleb Williams. They won the NFC North Division title last season and beat the rival Green Bay Packers in the first round of the playoffs before falling to the Los Angeles Rams.
The new movie will be filmed entirely in and around Chicago, Hallmark said. The cast will be announced at a later date.

title: How a Duck Named Merlin Became the Face of Team Mexico During the World Cup
summary: A duck wearing a Mexican jersey has become viral during the World Cup. Here is how he become the unofficial mascot for Team Mexico.
story: While the World Cup has brought the biggest names in soccer back into the international spotlight, one particular feathered fan is drawing just as many headlines.
Merlin, a 2-year-old duck, has taken the internet by storm, captivating fans with his tiny form-fitting Team Mexico jersey and confident waddle around his home of Mexico City.

The duck — who skyrocketed to internet fame in early June after he was seen reveling in the festivities following Mexico’s first victory in the tournament — has since become a proud symbol of the team, overshadowing FIFA’s official Mexico mascot, Zayu the Jaguar.

Merlin’s newfound celebrity status has taken him to new heights, landing him a visit with Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum. As Team Mexico’s third match of the tournament is slated for June 24, Merlin fever is likely to spread.
“I could have never imagined the magnitude of where my baby would get to,” owner Karla Gomez told NBC News. “To me, he is my son.”
How Did Merlin Become Famous?

Merlin, who has long been a fixture on the streets of Mexico City, first got a taste of fame after Team Mexico’s 2-0 victory against South Africa, the opening match of the World Cup, on June 11. Wearing not only the Mexico jersey, but also a pair of tiny socks, Merlin was captured on video, waddling around the bustling Reforma Avenue as fans celebrated the win.
Social media users, charmed by his Team Mexico spirit, began sharing memes across the internet, often pairing them with the phrase “Viva El Pato,” which translates to “Long Live The Duck!”
Some businesses have even capitalized off of the Merlin craze, with one store all the way in Canada modeling their jerseys on a row of display ducks.
Who Are Merlin’s Owners?
Gomez, Merlin’s owner, is a street beverage vendor in Mexico City. She previously told Reuters that the day Merlin went viral was like any other day for the family.
“We thought we were ​passing by unnoticed, because obviously we never thought Merlin would ‌have that boom,” she told the outlet.

The duck was initially given to Gomez as a gift by a customer two years ago, she said. The family decided to name the feathered friend Merlin after the mythical wizard because “since Merlin came into our lives, there have been many awesome changes in our world,” she told NBC News.
Merlin isn’t just a pet. He’s also an integral part of the family business, “inspecting” their work and even assuming the unofficial title of “boss,” according to the Agence France-Presse.

Merlin, who’s named after the mythical wizard, lives off a diet of fruit, veggies, crickets and fish, his owner told the AFP. Every so often, Gomez told the outlet, Merlin gets a celebratory taco too.
“He’s part of the family,” Gomez’s 14-year-old son Cristian Gomez told the AFP.
What Has Merlin Been up to Since He First Went Viral?

It seems everyone wants a piece of Merlin these days. Shortly after he climbed to superstardom, Merlin was named an official ambassador for his hometown of Mexico City, a host city for the Cup. He’s appeared on television programs and even joined Sheinbaum at her morning press conference on June 22.

“Today we brought the family that has Merlin the duck as their pet, because he has been a symbol of the World Cup, a symbol of ​what Mexican families ​stand for, of ⁠who we are as Mexican families, and that is above all else; it is what the world is seeing from ​Mexico today,” Sheinbaum said during the conference, according to Reuters.
Now, we’re just hoping that Merlin gets induckted into the International Football Hall of Fame. He deserves it.

title: Everyone’s Shipping Keke Palmer and Sean Evans. But Are They Actually Dating?
summary: Fans want Keke Palmer and Sean Evans to date. The two have been going viral for their flirty interactions with each other for years.
story: Keke Palmer and Sean Evans have been going viral for their flirty interactions with each other for years. But are the two actually dating?

The actor and the “Hot Ones” host were first linked together in November 2023 after Evans revealed during an appearance on Amelia Dimoldenberg’s “Chicken Shop Date” that Palmer was the one guest he has had a crush on. Palmer previously stopped by “Hot Ones” in 2017 and 2021.
Evans gushed about Palmer to Dimoldenberg, describing “The Burbs” star as a “very charming woman.”

Since then, Palmer appeared as a guest on “Hot Ones” again in September 2025, where the two shared a kiss. He, in turn, was interviewed by the actor on the May 26 episode of her “Baby, This is Keke Palmer” podcast, leading to comments about their undeniable chemistry on social media. A few weeks later, they were photographed getting dinner together.
While sitting on the panel for Spotify’s Conversations That Channel Culture talk at Cannes Lions on June 23, Palmer finally addressed their rumored romance.
